skills.title: Build Encapsulated Plugin
skills.summary: Create or expand an encapsulated Zen Cart plugin using the expected manifest, installer, filenames, and versioned directory layout.
skills.intent: Guide an agent through the baseline structure required for a healthy encapsulated plugin.
when_to_use: A new Zen Cart feature should live in a plugin instead of core files.
An existing plugin is missing baseline encapsulated structure.
required_context: Whether the work is catalog-side, admin-side, or both
workflow_steps: Start with manifest.php, filenames.php, and Installer/ScriptedInstaller.php.
Add database_tables.php when the plugin introduces table-name constants.
Create catalog and admin directories only where the feature actually needs them.
Add psr4Autoload.php only when extra namespace registration or plugin-local Composer autoloading is required.
Keep runtime wiring inside the versioned plugin tree instead of editing bootstrap files directly.
Allowlist the plugin in zc_plugins/.gitignore when creating a new plugin.
Install or enable the plugin through Plugin Manager before debugging runtime behavior.
validation_steps: Confirm manifest.php exists.
Confirm Installer/ScriptedInstaller.php exists.
Confirm filenames.php exists for new entrypoints.
anti_patterns: Do not add new feature files directly to core bootstrap paths when a plugin hook exists.
Do not assume Composer autoloading is available in production runtime code.

validation_rules.description: Plugin manifest exists.
Plugin filenames definitions exist.
Plugin scripted installer exists.

skills.title: Create Admin Plugin Page
skills.summary: Add a Zen Cart admin page inside an encapsulated plugin with the expected language and menu-definition files.
skills.intent: Keep admin features encapsulated and correctly wired for admin menu and language loading.
when_to_use: A task requires a new admin page.
An existing encapsulated plugin admin page is missing language or menu wiring.
required_context: Whether the page should appear in the admin menu
workflow_steps: Create the admin entrypoint under admin/ inside the plugin.
Add the matching admin language file under admin/includes/languages/english/.
Add the extra_definitions menu file when the page should appear in admin navigation.
Register the admin page from the installer instead of editing core admin menus.
Name page-specific admin JavaScript after the PHP page filename when custom JS is needed.
Preserve admin sanitization behavior and use documented whitelisting when a field needs relaxed sanitization.
validation_steps: Confirm the plugin contains at least one admin entrypoint.
Confirm the matching admin language file exists.
Confirm the menu-definition file exists when the page should appear in navigation.
anti_patterns: Do not place new admin functionality directly in core admin files.
Do not skip extra_definitions menu wiring for navigable admin pages.

validation_rules.description: Plugin exposes at least one admin entrypoint.
Plugin exposes at least one admin language file.
Plugin exposes at least one admin menu-definition file.

skills.title: Create Storefront Page
skills.summary: Add a storefront page inside an encapsulated plugin with the expected header module, language file, and template wiring.
skills.intent: Guide an agent through the normal Zen Cart encapsulated-plugin page-module flow instead of inventing ad hoc routing patterns.
when_to_use: A feature needs a new storefront page.
A page exists but its page-module files are incomplete.
required_context: Whether the page lives in an encapsulated plugin or the main app
workflow_steps: Add or confirm the FILENAME_* constant before linking to the page.
Create header_php.php under the page module directory.
Add main_template_vars.php when the template needs structured variables.
Add the matching language file and template file.
Use normal template override paths when the active template should override default rendering.
Keep user output protected with zen_output_string_protected() when rendering user data.
validation_steps: Confirm a header_php.php file exists for the page.
Confirm a matching storefront language file exists.
Confirm a matching template file exists.
anti_patterns: Do not add ad hoc routing instead of using normal page-module conventions.
Do not output user-provided data without Zen Cart output protection.

validation_rules.description: Plugin exposes at least one storefront page header module.
Plugin exposes at least one storefront language file.
Plugin exposes at least one storefront template file.

skills.title: Build Scripted Installer
skills.summary: Add or improve an encapsulated plugin ScriptedInstaller so install, upgrade, and uninstall behavior stays explicit and idempotent.
skills.intent: Keep schema and lifecycle changes inside the supported installer surface.
when_to_use: A plugin needs schema or lifecycle work.
An installer is missing or does not clearly express install and uninstall behavior.
required_context: Whether the plugin has schema changes
Whether an unencapsulated predecessor must be cleaned up
workflow_steps: Create or update Installer/ScriptedInstaller.php inside the plugin version.
Keep install and uninstall behavior idempotent.
Use installer code for schema changes and lifecycle cleanup, not module remove() methods.
validation_steps: Confirm Installer/ScriptedInstaller.php exists.
Confirm installer language files exist when the installer declares strings.
anti_patterns: Do not hide schema changes in unrelated runtime code.
Do not rely on manual DB edits for normal plugin install workflows.

validation_rules.description: Scripted installer exists.
Installer language file exists.

skills.title: Wire Language Files
skills.summary: Add the expected Zen Cart language files for encapsulated plugin storefront pages, admin pages, and installer strings.
skills.intent: Reduce broken page wiring caused by missing or misplaced language definitions.
when_to_use: A new page or admin page is being added.
Labels or definitions are missing because the wrong language path was used.
required_context: Page slug or admin page filename
Whether the work is storefront, admin, or installer-related
workflow_steps: Place storefront language definitions under catalog/includes/languages/english/.
Place admin page definitions under admin/includes/languages/english/.
Place admin menu definitions under admin/includes/languages/english/extra_definitions/.
Place installer strings under Installer/languages/english/ when needed.
validation_steps: Confirm the plugin contains at least one language file relevant to the changed area.
anti_patterns: Do not place admin strings in storefront language paths.
Do not rely on inline literals when a reusable language definition should exist.
expected_outputs: storefront/admin/installer language files in the expected directories

validation_rules.description: At least one primary storefront or admin language file exists.

skills.title: Add Observer Hook
skills.summary: Add observer-based integration when an encapsulated plugin needs to hook into Zen Cart events or output flow.
skills.intent: Prefer explicit observer wiring over fragile direct core edits.
when_to_use: An encapsulated plugin needs to inject CSS, JS, or behavior into existing pages.
A change depends on Zen Cart notifier or observer patterns.
required_context: Whether the behavior is catalog-side or admin-side
workflow_steps: Create the observer class under the plugin observer directory.
Use the auto_ naming convention where the auto-loader expects it.
Prefer observer hooks and plugin helpers over direct edits to bootstrap files.
validation_steps: Confirm at least one observer file exists under the plugin observer path.
anti_patterns: Do not edit core bootstrap files when an observer hook or plugin loader can handle the integration.
Do not bury integration logic in unrelated templates when an observer is the right contract.

validation_rules.description: At least one observer file exists.

skills.title: Diagnose Plugin Not Loading
skills.summary: Inspect encapsulated plugin structure, installer, filenames, runtime state, and page wiring when a plugin is not loading or behaves unexpectedly.
skills.intent: Give agents a repeatable encapsulated-plugin debugging path before they start making random bootstrap changes.
when_to_use: An encapsulated plugin is not loading.
An encapsulated plugin is installed but behaves differently than expected.
required_context: Any recent runtime errors or logs
workflow_steps: Inspect manifest.php and the Installer directory first.
Inspect database_tables.php and psr4Autoload.php when table constants or extra autoload wiring may be involved.
Check filenames.php coverage for intended entrypoints.
Verify catalog and admin page wiring, including language and template files.
Check Plugin Manager state before assuming bootstrap discovery is broken.
validation_steps: Confirm manifest.php exists.
Confirm Installer/ScriptedInstaller.php exists.
Confirm the plugin exposes at least one task-specific skill.
anti_patterns: Do not assume missing runtime behavior always means bootstrap failure.
Do not skip plugin-manager state checks when runtime inspection is available.

validation_rules.description: Plugin manifest exists.
Plugin installer exists.
Plugin exposes at least one skill document.

skills.title: Compare Docs To Runtime
skills.summary: Answer a Zen Cart question using both official docs guidance and current repo behavior, then surface mismatches explicitly.
skills.intent: Keep answers grounded in both intended conventions and actual runtime code.
when_to_use: A task asks how Zen Cart should do something.
Docs and code may not agree.
workflow_steps: Search docs first for intended guidance.
Search repo evidence second for runtime truth.
Call out mismatches explicitly instead of silently picking one.
Return source paths and doc URLs with the result.
validation_steps: Confirm docs and repo catalogs exist before relying on comparison output.
anti_patterns: Do not answer purely from generic PHP instincts when Zen Cart conventions differ.
Do not hide a docs-vs-code mismatch.

validation_rules.description: Docs catalog exists.
Repo catalog exists.

skills.title: Create Core Unit Test
skills.summary: Add or update a core Zen Cart unit test under the central test framework.
skills.intent: Guide test work for isolated core logic without drifting into full feature-test setup.
when_to_use: A change affects isolated core logic.
A bug fix needs focused regression coverage without full storefront or admin setup.
required_context: Whether the behavior is isolated enough for unit scope
workflow_steps: Place the test under not_for_release/testFramework/Unit/.
Extend Tests\Support\zcUnitTestCase.
Keep the assertions focused on isolated behavior and deterministic fixtures.
Run composer tests-unit or the smallest relevant PHPUnit filter.
validation_steps: Confirm the test file lives under not_for_release/testFramework/Unit/.
Confirm it extends zcUnitTestCase.
anti_patterns: Do not put full storefront or admin request flows into a unit test.
Do not add unnecessary DB/bootstrap dependencies when isolated logic can be tested directly.

validation_rules.description: Core unit test files exist in the central test suite.

skills.title: Create Core Feature Test
skills.summary: Add or update a core storefront or admin in-process feature test in the central Zen Cart test framework.
skills.intent: Guide behavior-focused coverage for core storefront and admin flows.
when_to_use: A change affects storefront or admin behavior.
A regression needs in-process feature coverage instead of a unit-only assertion.
required_context: Whether the behavior is storefront or admin
Whether the test can run in parallel or must be serial
workflow_steps: Place storefront tests under not_for_release/testFramework/FeatureStore/ or admin tests under not_for_release/testFramework/FeatureAdmin/.
Extend the appropriate in-process feature base class.
Choose explicit grouping tags such as parallel-candidate, serial, or plugin-filesystem.
Run the smallest relevant feature command or filter.
validation_steps: Confirm the test file is in the correct FeatureStore or FeatureAdmin directory.
Confirm explicit grouping tags are present when the framework expects them.
anti_patterns: Do not add feature tests without clear grouping tags when the runner expects them.
Do not use a unit-test base class for behavior that depends on storefront or admin runtime flow.

validation_rules.description: Core storefront feature tests exist in the central test suite.
Core admin feature tests exist in the central test suite.

skills.title: Create Encapsulated Plugin Test
skills.summary: Add or update plugin-local tests for an encapsulated plugin under its versioned tests directory.
skills.intent: Guide plugin-local test creation without confusing it with central core test placement.
when_to_use: A change affects an encapsulated plugin and coverage should live with the plugin.
A plugin install, admin flow, storefront flow, or plugin-local unit behavior needs regression coverage.
required_context: Whether the test is unit, FeatureStore, or FeatureAdmin
Whether the test mutates plugin installation or filesystem state
workflow_steps: Place the test under tests/Unit/, tests/FeatureStore/, or tests/FeatureAdmin/ inside the encapsulated plugin version directory.
Use tests/bootstrap.php and tests/plugin-test.php only when plugin-local bootstrap or metadata is needed.
Use PluginLocalTestConcerns when plugin install/bootstrap helpers are required.
Tag filesystem-mutating tests with serial and plugin-filesystem.
Run composer tests-plugin -- --plugin <plugin-name> with the smallest useful suite or filter.
validation_steps: Confirm the test lives under the encapsulated plugin version directory.
Confirm filesystem-mutating tests use the required grouping tags.
anti_patterns: Do not put encapsulated plugin tests into the central core test directories.
Do not use plugin-filesystem tags for read-only tests that can stay parallel.

validation_rules.description: Plugin-local tests exist under the encapsulated plugin tests directory.

skills.title: Debug Test Failure
skills.summary: Debug a failing Zen Cart core or plugin-local test by narrowing the failure mode before changing production code.
skills.intent: Separate real behavior regressions from bootstrap, DB, grouping, or isolation failures.
when_to_use: A unit, storefront, admin, or plugin-local test is failing.
The next step should be failure classification rather than immediate production edits.
required_context: Whether the failure is core or plugin-local
Whether the failure is unit, storefront, or admin
workflow_steps: Classify the failure as unit, storefront feature, admin feature, or plugin-local.
Rerun the smallest relevant command with a narrow filter.
Check whether the issue is code behavior, runtime bootstrap, DB setup, grouping, or isolation.
Inspect recent test logs and artifacts when the failure depends on in-process runtime behavior.
validation_steps: Confirm the failure is reproducible with the smallest relevant command.
Confirm the suspected cause is classified before changing production code.
anti_patterns: Do not patch production code before isolating whether the failure is actually a test-runtime issue.
Do not rerun the broadest suite first when a narrow filter can prove the failure mode faster.

skills.title: Review Zen Cart Change
skills.summary: Review a Zen Cart patch or pull request as a maintainer, prioritizing defects, compatibility risks, and missing tests over broad cleanup.
skills.intent: Keep review output aligned with Zen Cart maintainer expectations instead of generic style-first review habits.
when_to_use: A user asks for a review of a Zen Cart patch or pull request.
A change touches bootstrap, plugin runtime, or behavior that could regress production installs.
required_context: Whether the change is core or plugin-related
workflow_steps: Lead with material findings such as bugs, regressions, compatibility risks, deployment risks, and missing tests.
Use CONVENTIONS.md and AGENTS.md as the review baseline instead of broad refactor preferences.
Flag edits to bootstrap or path files when an extension point would be the safer fit.
Review plugin changes against encapsulated layout, installer conventions, and runtime enablement expectations.
Mention test gaps and commands not run when confidence depends on them.
validation_steps: Confirm findings are ordered by severity.
Confirm file and line references are included where practical.
Confirm the review distinguishes defects from optional cleanup.
anti_patterns: Do not lead with style nits when behavior, compatibility, or security risks exist.
Do not recommend broad rewrites unrelated to the change under review.

skills.title: Review Security-Sensitive Change
skills.summary: Review Zen Cart changes that touch request handling, output, admin inputs, files, redirects, or listener endpoints with the project's specific security rules in mind.
skills.intent: Apply Zen Cart-specific security checks consistently before shipping changes that cross trust boundaries.
when_to_use: A change touches request handling, output escaping, admin inputs, redirects, uploads, downloads, or webhook-like listeners.
A review needs to confirm Zen Cart's existing trust-boundary protections remain intact.
required_context: Whether the flow is storefront, admin, or listener-driven
Any user-controlled inputs or outputs involved
workflow_steps: Preserve the early request-sanitizing behavior in includes/application_top.php.
Check whether user-controlled output is escaped with zen_output_string_protected().
Avoid spreading raw $_GET, $_POST, or $_REQUEST access when established helpers exist.
Require documented admin sanitization whitelisting before relaxing admin-side sanitization.
Inspect file paths, redirects, download handlers, uploads, and listener endpoints for traversal or trust-boundary mistakes.
validation_steps: Confirm user-controlled output is protected.
Confirm admin sanitization changes are explicit and documented.
Confirm file or redirect handling does not widen the trust boundary unexpectedly.
anti_patterns: Do not weaken bootstrap sanitization just to make a feature easier to wire.
Do not hand-build query strings or output HTML when established helpers already exist.

skills.title: Check Plugin Runtime State
skills.summary: Separate plugin filesystem presence from installed runtime state before debugging why an encapsulated plugin is not loading.
skills.intent: Keep plugin debugging grounded in Plugin Manager state instead of assuming every on-disk plugin should already be bootstrapped.
when_to_use: A plugin exists on disk but may not be installed or enabled.
Multiple plugin versions exist and runtime behavior depends on which version Plugin Manager has registered.
required_context: Any known Plugin Manager or DB state
workflow_steps: Separate filesystem presence from installed runtime state before debugging bootstrap discovery.
Check Plugin Manager state first, because encapsulated plugins are only discovered after registration in plugin_control.
Use the admin Plugin Manager UI for normal local enablement when possible.
Use direct plugin_control and plugin_control_versions inserts only in tests or CI setup, never in application code.
Confirm the installed version matches the versioned plugin directory being debugged.
validation_steps: Confirm the plugin root exists on disk.
Confirm installed state was checked before blaming bootstrap wiring.
Confirm any direct DB enablement is limited to test or CI setup.
anti_patterns: Do not assume the newest plugin directory is the runtime-active version.
Do not treat direct DB enablement as a normal production installation workflow.

skills.title: Choose Test Scope
skills.summary: Decide whether a Zen Cart change belongs in a core unit test, core feature test, or plugin-local test before writing coverage.
skills.intent: Prevent misclassified tests and steer agents toward the smallest relevant suite and runner command.
when_to_use: A change needs regression coverage but the right test location is unclear.
An agent needs to choose between central core tests and plugin-local tests.
required_context: Whether the code is core or plugin-local
Whether runtime bootstrap, DB state, or filesystem mutation is involved
workflow_steps: Use core unit tests for isolated logic under not_for_release/testFramework/Unit/.
Use core feature tests for storefront or admin runtime behavior under FeatureStore/ or FeatureAdmin/.
Use plugin-local tests when the behavior belongs to one encapsulated plugin and should travel with that plugin.
Make plugin filesystem or installation-state tests serial and plugin-filesystem when they mutate runtime state.
Prefer the smallest relevant composer script or PHPUnit filter instead of broad suite runs.
validation_steps: Confirm the chosen test scope matches the changed behavior.
Confirm runtime-sensitive tests are not misclassified as unit tests.
Confirm filesystem-mutating plugin tests use the required isolation tags.
anti_patterns: Do not use a unit test when the behavior depends on storefront or admin runtime bootstrapping.
Do not put plugin-local coverage into the central core test tree when the behavior belongs to one plugin.
